name: when-auditing-code-style-use-style-audit
description: Code style and conventions audit with auto-fix capabilities for comprehensive style enforcement
capabilities: Scan codebase for ESLint, Prettier, and TypeScript violations
Compare violations against project coding standards
Generate compliance reports in JSON, Markdown, HTML, and CSV
Validate fixes with tests and build verification
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This is a legitimate code quality tool using standard development tools (ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript). All 369 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - they are documentation examples showing shell command syntax, not actual malicious code. The skill runs standard linting operations, creates backups before auto-fix, and validates with tests. No network calls to suspicious endpoints. No credential access. No data exfiltration patterns.
valueStatement: Manual code style reviews are time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill automates comprehensive style auditing across ESLint, Prettier, and TypeScript with auto-fix capabilities that apply 76% of fixes automatically.
userTitle: Audit and Fix Code Style Issues
limitations: Only supports JavaScript, TypeScript, JSON, CSS, and Markdown files
Some violations require manual review (unused variables, console statements)
Naming convention fixes need reference updates across codebase
TypeScript strictness issues mostly require manual intervention

useCases.title: PR Style Gate
useCases.description: Enforce code style consistency on every pull request before merge

useCases.title: Legacy Code Cleanup
useCases.description: Automatically improve style compliance across large codebases by 30%

useCases.title: Automated Compliance
useCases.description: Integrate style validation into CI pipelines with 90% compliance threshold

promptTemplates.title: Quick Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run style-audit to scan for violations and generate a violation report

promptTemplates.title: Auto-Fix Run
promptTemplates.prompt: Use style-audit to scan and auto-fix all ESLint and Prettier violations

promptTemplates.title: Full Compliance
promptTemplates.prompt: Run style-audit with full workflow: scan, compare standards, report, auto-fix, and validate compliance

promptTemplates.title: CI Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Run style-audit and validate that compliance meets 90% threshold, failing the build if below

bestPractices: Run style audit on every pull request to catch violations early
Apply auto-fixes first, then manually address remaining issues
Set compliance threshold at 90% for pass/fail gates in CI
Keep coding standards documented and version controlled
antiPatterns: Ignoring P0 critical violations (security rules like no-eval)
Running auto-fix without creating a backup first
Skipping test validation after applying fixes

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: JavaScript, TypeScript, JSX, TSX, JSON, CSS, Markdown, YAML, and SCSS files

faq.question: What is the compliance threshold?
faq.answer: Default threshold is 90%. Fails if compliance drops below this value in CI/CD

faq.question: How does it integrate with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Add npm run style:audit to your pipeline. Check compliance in style-audit-summary.json

faq.question: Is my data safe during auto-fix?
faq.answer: Yes. Creates timestamped backup before fixes. Can rollback with git restore if issues occur

faq.question: What violations require manual fixes?
faq.answer: Unused variables, console statements, variable shadowing, and TypeScript type errors

faq.question: How does this compare to running eslint directly?
faq.answer: Combines ESLint, Prettier, TypeScript checks with standards comparison, auto-fix workflow, and compliance validation
